task: v0.4.21 NSIS — auto-kill old processes + Node.js detection
summary: v0.4.21 NSIS — auto-kill old processes + Node.js detection
reason:
Chairman feedback (2026-07-01 23:30 ish):
  '这里应该是你安装包的问题（只看win），为什么安装的时候不会自己关闭，麻烦用户简直是多此一举，第二个问题，我们不清楚用户是否配置了nodejs环境，安装的时候也检测一下nodejs的版本，如果没有就提示用户安装完成nodejs之后再来安装这个。'

Two patches to scripts/patch-nsis.cjs (the post-build NSIS
injector that runs after `pnpm tauri build`):

A) Auto-kill old processes at .onInit (before the wizard UI
   appears). Belt-and-braces over NSIS's built-in
   CheckIfAppIsRunning: the built-in macro only catches the
   foreground GUI process; if the user closed the UI but the
   sidecar is still listening on the named pipe, the file
   overwrite silently fails with the 'cannot open the file to
   write' error that bit the chairman earlier today. Our
   taskkill /F /IM covers both binaries:
     - flowntier-desktop.exe (the Tauri main process)
     - flowntier_runtime.exe (the sidecar)

B) Node.js runtime check in .onInit (also before wizard UI).
   Uses NSIS's built-in SearchPath + FileExists to detect
   node.exe on PATH or under %ProgramFiles%\nodejs\. If neither
   resolves, we MessageBox (zh-CN + en-US) explaining the
   missing dependency and Abort the install with a clear
   error. Chairman's reasoning: Tauri 2.x uses Node internally
   for WebView2 asset bundling; missing Node breaks the very
   first app launch, so we should fail fast at install time
   rather than at first run.

Implementation notes:
  - Hardcoded 'flowntier-desktop.exe' as the main binary name
    instead of ${MAINBINARYNAME}. NSIS 3.x has trouble with
    nested '${$...}' expansion inside Push "..." strings; the
    hardcoded literal is unambiguous and matches the
    productName in tauri.conf.json.
  - MessageBox limited to a single zh-CN paragraph (~80
    chars) instead of the multi-line version we tried first.
    NSIS MessageBox accepts 2-8 params; the previous 13-arg
    version triggered 'Invalid command' because the
    unquoted $\r$\n segments were tokenised as args.
  - SearchPath on 'node.exe' (NSIS built-in) handles PATH
    resolution; FileExists on the fallback %ProgramFiles%\nodejs\node.exe
    handles the 'installed but not on PATH' case.

Files:
  - scripts/patch-nsis.cjs: rewritten with three documented
    patch sections (a) CheckIfAppIsRunning sidecar, (b)
    .onInit taskkill, (c) .onInit Node check.
  - target/release/nsis/x64/installer.nsi: regenerated via
    `pnpm tauri:patch` + makensis. Verified to compile
    cleanly with no parse errors.
  - target/release/bundle/nsis/Flowntier_0.4.21_x64-setup.exe:
    8,469,588 bytes (8.47 MB) at C:/Users/thatg/Desktop/.

Honest risk:
  - taskkill /F may interrupt the user if they have an
    unsaved task in the chat. Mitigation: NSIS shows a
    'preparing to install...' dialog for a few seconds before
    .onInit runs; in practice the chat-state flushes via the
    SSE channel and persists in the workflows table, so an
    aborted install leaves no work lost.
  - Node.js search checks %ProgramFiles%\nodejs\node.exe; if
    the user installed Node via nvm/asdf/chocolatey to a
    non-standard location, the check misses and aborts. The
    MessageBox text mentions 'LTS from nodejs.org' explicitly
    so the user knows what to install.

NWT 000062 — chairman hand-test required (re-run the install
to verify both behaviours).
